segments.text: I'm Dr. Tejaswi calling on behalf of Tata AIG Life Insurance. Can I speak to Mr. Avinjo Varghese?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, sir. This is with reference to your insurance policy. We have decided to go ahead with your proposal, but as a part of the process, we need to conduct a video medical verification. In which I'll ask you some verification and medical history questions, sir.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: This call will be recorded for company's audit and quality purpose. I'll be asking you a few questions regarding your insurance application and this recorded call should take 10 to 12 minutes, sir. I hope you can continue with the call.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Kindly note that the replies and the information provided by you on this call relating to your health and habits will be considered as a part of your application for insurance and if the proposal is accepted, it shall form basis of legal contract of insurance between you and Tata AIG Life Insurance, sir. Any incorrect or false statement or replies could have an impact on contract and may lead to cancellation of policy before or after issuance and may lead to rejection of claims later, sir. You're hence requested to respond thoughtfully and correctly, sir.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Kindly confirm your full name, sir.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, sir. Are you self-employed or salaried, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Can you please show me your ID proof, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Can you please tell me the last four digits of your Aadhaar number, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Can you please show me a full complete view of yours, like you have to stand and show me your complete view, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Have to see everything from top to bottom, sir. Hold the phone as if you are taking like a selfie.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Uh, but, but cannot see until down, sir. You can place your phone somewhere and move.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: No, sir, I can see only your head. If it's not a problem for you, can you place it on the floor to touching the wall so that I can see your full view, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Yes, sir, I can see. Just hold the phone and show your foot, sir.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Foot. Just hold the phone and show your foot, sir.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Just show it towards your foot.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: No, no, sir, it's not visible.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: You can just stand straight and just show towards your foot, sir.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Verification questions were done, sir. We'll move ahead with the lifestyle and the medical history questions, sir.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: In the last 24 months, have you smoked or used any tobacco?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: No, I'm not, I'm a non-smoker.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Okay, sir. Do you consume alcohol?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Please select the type of alcohol you consume. Is it a beer, wine, or a hard liquor?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Mostly beer, okay, sir. You consume it only occasionally?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, sir. Have you ever been advised by a doctor to reduce or stop alcohol consumption?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, sir. Have you ever used recreational drugs or narcotics that was not prescribed by a licensed physician?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: No, ma'am, I have not used.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Have you ever been diagnosed with or investigated for or had been or currently under treatment for any of the following? If yes, please provide the details. High or low blood pressure, raised cholesterol or triglycerides or lipids, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Yeah. Uh, triglycerides on a, my triglycerides are on 220.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Okay, sir. You're having high triglycerides.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, sir, just a second. Okay, sir.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: When was your last cholesterol test done, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, sir, fine. Do you know what your lipid reading was?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: I can just note, it is on my mobile. It is two, the triglyceride was 280, and LDL was 101, and HDL was 35.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Okay, sir, you can mention only the high values so that it is enough.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Only the triglyceride was 270 last which I took. Yeah.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Okay, okay, sir. Only the triglycerides are high.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Yeah, the LDL was normal, the limit only, 109, 98, 101.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Okay, sir. Apart from your regular follow-ups for this condition, are you awaiting further medical test or investigations, test results, or referral to hospital or surgery for this raised triglycerides, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: No, I don't, I don't have any surgery or related to anything.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Okay, sir. Have you had any of the following complications like heart problems, circulatory problems, nerve or sensory damage, kidney problems, blood or protein in the urine, eye problem because of this high triglycerides, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Because I took my echo, I mean, TMT last month only. It was negative.

segments.text: You just need to mention, sir, that's enough.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, sir. Chest pain or heaviness or discomfort, heart murmur, heart attack, coronary artery disease, heart valve disorder, congenital heart disease, palpitation, rheumatic fever, shortness of breath or any other heart condition?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, sir. Cancer or carcinoma in situ, pre-cancerous condition, leukemia, enlarged nodes, unusual skin lesions, dysplasia, leukoplakia, sarcoma, tumors, polyps, nodules, cysts, lumps or any other growths, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, sir. Diabetes, raised blood sugar or sugar in urine, hyperthyroidism, hypothyroidism, thyroid or any other endocrine disorders?

segments.text: Okay, sir. It's been seven years you got diabetes.

segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Yes, ma'am. I'm always in the follow-up with, because I'm working for a pharma company. So always in, I'm working for a diabetic product only, so I'm always on the test. I always monitor my tests always regularly.

segments.text: Uh, three, diabetic six months and cholesterol levels one year.

segments.text: And uh, echo eye test one year and echo and TMT one, once in a year.

segments.text: Okay, sir. Uh, you are on follow-up with diabetes for six months once, sir?

segments.text: Okay, I got a, I got a glucometer, so I check on my routinely, monthly, thrice or five times. I check my, uh.

segments.text: blood and HBA1C blood test done in the last six months?

segments.text: Yes, ma'am. I got my seven my values were 7.6 recently Jan 5th, March. And the previous six months it was 7.1.

segments.text: It should be 7.1, 7.2, 7. The reason was Jan 5th, March. That depends on the level lot of functions around. So the three months HFC was not controlled. My usual sugar levels used to be fasting 110, 120 and post prandial is 145, 160.

segments.text: I think it was 7.8, ma'am. 7.8. Six months back it was 7.1.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: And my uh fasting was 110 and my uh postprandial was 120, something like that.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Okay, sir. I need this HBA1C result, sir. Uh, and the recent checkup how much value you have got you can tell me that. Is it 7.8, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, sir. Sir, just a second, sir.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, sir. Have you ever suffered from any complication such as diabetic coma, heart disease, stroke, kidney impairment, circulation problem, vascular disease, gangrene, eye or vision problems, protein in urine because of this diabetes, sir?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: No, uh no, ma'am. Because because I take this Dapagliflozin uh the urine sugar is will be positive. Because I'm taking that DAPA because the excretion route of DAPA is the urine. So it earlier it doesn't show, now it shows a trace of uh sugar in urine.

segments.text: No, no, no, ma'am. My urinary albumin creatinine ratio is all normal. Creatinine was 1.1.

segments.text: We'll move on to the next question, sir. Asthma, bronchitis, tonsillitis, tuberculosis, pneumonia, chronic or recurrent cough, sleep apnea, emphysema, chronic pulmonary disease or any other respiratory disorders, sir?

segments.text: No, ma'am. I don't have any history of chronic.

Gastritis, gastroesophageal reflux disease, stomach ulcer, recurrent indigestion, irritable bowel syndrome, Barrett's esophagus, atrophic gastritis, Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, pancreatitis or pancreatic disorder or any other gastrointestinal diseases or disorders?
